{{Quote|Drop your weapons.|3B3, to the Jedi|<ref name="Mighty">[[The Phantom Menace (Mighty Chronicles)|''The Phantom Menace'' Mighty Chronicles adaptation]]</ref>}}
'''3B3''' was a [[B-series battle droid/Legends|B1 battle droid]] that served in the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]]. During the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], 3B3 discovered the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] [[Capture of Theed/Legends|sneaking]] into the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. 3B3 ordered them to drop their weapons but was swiftly destroyed by Jinn.

==Biography==
3B3 was a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] that served in the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="Obi" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> 3B3 participated in the [[Capture of Theed/Legends|capture]] of Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. During the battle, 3B3 encountered the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who had traveled to Theed via the [[Solleu River/Legends|Solleu River]] to rescue Naboo's [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|queen]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. They also ended up needing to pull their [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] companion [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] out by an [[Ascension gun/Legends|ascension gun]] cable connected to their [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|tribubble bongo]].<ref name="Obi" />

3B3 ordered the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to stop and aimed a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] at them. Jinn turned<ref name="Obi" /> and quickly<ref name="Illustrated">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay'']]</ref> yet casually<ref name="Mighty">[[The Phantom Menace (Mighty Chronicles)|''The Phantom Menace'' Mighty Chronicles adaptation]]</ref> decapitated the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] with his [[Qui-Gon Jinn's lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] while 3B3 simultaneously fired its blaster, hitting the grappling hook cable and sending the bongo over a [[Water/Legends|waterfall]].<ref name="Obi" /> Kenobi looked at the droid's body as it lay to the ground.<ref name="Danger">''[[Dangers of the Core]]''</ref>

==Characteristics==
3B3's name was a shortened version of its unique identification code, which was usually applied by Trade Federation [[Technician/Legends|technicians]].<ref name="Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> Like all B1 battle droids, 3B3 stood at 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, had [[Gender/Legends|masculine programming]],<ref name="Clone Wars" /> and had [[Color/Legends|tan]] plating.<ref name="Obi" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Hold it right there.|3B3's alternate dialogue in ''Episode I: Obi-Wan Kenobi''|<ref name="Obi" />}}
3B3 was created for the [[prequel trilogy]] film {{Film|I}}, written by [[George Lucas]]. The droid appeared in drafts of the screenplay but was cut from the finished film,<ref name="Illustrated" /> which premiered on [[May 19]], [[1999]].<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> 3B3's first appearance was in the picture book ''[[Dangers of the Core]]'', an adaptation of a portion of ''The Phantom Menace'' that was written by [[Jim Thomas]], illustrated by [[Boris Vallejo]],<ref name="Danger" /> and published in [[April]] 1999.<ref name="Date">{{RandomHouseOld|url=catalog/display.pperl?isbn=0375800026|text=Dangers of the Core}}</ref>

[[File:3B3_Danger.png|thumb|right|300px|3B3 points a blaster at Jar Jar Binks in ''Dangers of the Core''.]]
3B3's encounter with the Jedi is portrayed in slightly different ways across adaptations of ''The Phantom Menace''.  In ''Dangers of the Core'', 3B3 arrives and is pictured standing in the Solleu River just as Kenobi pulls Jinn ashore. After Binks arrives, 3B3 points a blaster at him before being bisected by Jinn.<ref name="Danger" /> In the [[The Phantom Menace (Mighty Chronicles)|Mighty Chronicles]] adaptation, written by [[John Whitman]]<ref name="Mighty" /> and published on [[May 3]], 1999,<ref name="Amazon Mighty">{{Chronicle|old=1|url=cgi-bin/Adult/view.cgi?isbn=0811823156|text=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace}}</ref> 3B3 runs into the Jedi on a riverbank just outside Theed rather than inside the city itself.<ref name="Mighty" /> In ''[[Episode I: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', a one-shot [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[Henry Gilroy]] and illustrated by [[Martin Egeland]],<ref name="Obi" /> and published on [[June 2]], 1999,<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/98-326/Star-Wars-Episode-I---Obi-Wan-Kenobi|text=Star Wars: Episode I - Obi-Wan Kenobi}}</ref> the droid tells the Jedi to "hold it"<ref name="Obi" /> rather than to drop their weapons as portrayed in other sources.<ref name="Danger" /><ref name="Illustrated" />

The [[Content cut from Star Wars|deleted]] scene in which Jinn, Kenobi, and Binks climb out of the Solleu River was included in the [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] of ''The Phantom Menace'',<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}} [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]]</ref> released on [[October 16]], [[2001]],<ref name="DVD date">{{SWArchive|url=episode-i/news/2001/06/news20010619.html|text=DVD: The Wait is Over}}</ref> but 3B3 did not appear in the sequence.<ref name="TPM" />
